label: short
chain_of_thought:
Okay

We are asked to compute:

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor
$$

Let's compute each term:

### 1. $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor$

$$
\frac{1000}{7} = 142.857\ldots \Rightarrow \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor = 142
$$

### 2. $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor$

$$
\frac{1000}{11} = 90.909\ldots \Rightarrow \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor = 90
$$

### 3. $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor$

$$
\frac{1000}{13} = 76.923\ldots \Rightarrow \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor = 76
$$

### Final sum:

$$
142 + 90 + 76 = \boxed{308}
$$
